title: US-Iran Indirect Talks Held in Muscat, Oman
detail: The United States and Iran held their first indirect talks since the June 2025 strikes, mediated by Omani Foreign Minister Badr bin Hamad Al Busaidi in Muscat. The talks occurred in two stages: Iran presented its proposal through Oman, then received the US response. Iran insisted discussions focus exclusively on the nuclear file, while the US sought a comprehensive framework covering missiles, proxies, and human rights. Oman's foreign minister hailed 'significant progress,' though neither side shifted from its opening position.

title: CENTCOM Commander Joins US Negotiating Team
detail: US Central Command chief Admiral Brad Cooper participated in the Muscat talks alongside envoy Witkoff and Kushner, the first time a senior military commander was included in US-Iran nuclear diplomacy. Cooper's presence underscored the military pressure underpinning the diplomatic track, with the USS Abraham Lincoln and growing air assets in the region serving as implicit leverage. Iranian officials noted the inclusion as evidence of US coercive intent.

title: Rubio Casts Doubt on Iran Deal Prospects
detail: Secretary of State Marco Rubio publicly expressed skepticism about the Muscat talks, stating 'I'm not sure you can reach a deal with these guys.' Vice President J.D. Vance echoed frustration that Supreme Leader Khamenei did not participate directly in negotiations, questioning whether any agreement could hold without his involvement. The statements contrasted with Oman's positive assessment, signaling deep divisions within the diplomatic process.
